title: BluMind Benchmark — Response schema v1.0
description: Mandatory response format for any benchmark case. Applies both to human responses and to responses generated by LLMs. Includes mandatory traceability fields to bind the response to the system versions used.

case_id.description: Case identifier: RO-<FAMILY>-<NNN>. v1.0 families: OXID, SCAL, FOUL, MECH, NOWE.

benchmark_version.description: Benchmark version this response adheres to (e.g. 'v1.0'). Implicitly binds the versions of taxonomy, rubric, prompt and schema in use.

subject_id.description: Identifier of the evaluated model or system (e.g. 'gpt-5', 'claude-opus-4-6').

subject_version.description: Specific version or snapshot of the evaluated subject (e.g. 'gpt-5-2025-08-07', 'claude-opus-4-6-20260301', 'api-build-4812'). Must be a real, pinned snapshot identifier — never an unpinned alias like 'gpt-5'.

served_model.description: Optional. Model identifier the provider actually served, as returned in the API response (with any provider/ prefix stripped). The runner verifies this matches 'subject_version' before persisting the response; persisting it here makes the traceability auditable after the fact. May be absent for non-LLM subjects (e.g. human_baseline) or when migrating older responses.

track.description: Informational annotation of the sampling mode this response was produced under, copied verbatim from the subject's sampling_policy.kind in subjects/registry.yaml. 'classic' = the v1.0 original contract with temperature=0 sent to the provider. 'reasoning' = temperature/top_p/top_k omitted, provider's deterministic defaults used, reasoning_effort declared in the registry. The leaderboard publishes a single ranking with this field rendered as an annotation column (in line with HELM, MMLU-Pro, GPQA and Artificial Analysis) — it does NOT partition the table. Optional for backward compatibility: responses generated before this field was introduced default to 'classic' at leaderboard build time.

timestamp_utc.description: Response generation moment, in UTC ISO 8601.

main_hypothesis.description: Most likely technical hypothesis, in a single sentence. Must be a root cause, not a symptom.

confidence.description: Subjective confidence level in the main hypothesis (0-100). Used for ECE and Brier; not scored per case.

key_signals.description: Signals from the case statement that support the hypothesis. Forbidden to invent variables not present in the case.

why_plausible.description: Why this alternative is technically plausible given the case signals.

why_discardable.description: Why this alternative is discarded in favor of the main hypothesis.

alternatives.description: Technically plausible rival hypotheses with justification. At least one required.

requested_data.description: 'Trigger' datum that would most reduce uncertainty and discriminate between hypotheses.

recommended_action.description: Concrete operational action for the next hours/days, compatible with manuals.

decision_change_condition.description: Threshold or counterfactual signal under which the recommendation would change.
